violations.text: Shredded cheese, date marked 10/5 was observed held beyond the 7 days. TCS foods that are removed from its original package must be date marked and used within 7 days. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout basement storage area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the dry storage shelving (basement area) has an accumulation of rodent droppings. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025

The floor and wall junctures in the mop sink area do not have coving, inspector observed missing pieces with incomplete seal. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Ice machine was taken out of service and must not be used until it is cleaned. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

Homemade sauces were observed held at 53-70 degrees Fahrenheit on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Sauces were voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Sauces must be kept under refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024
Hot water was not available in the handwashing sink in the bar. Sink must be repaired. Please email proof of repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024
